acceptance_criteria.when: a reader inspects the dispatch payload body in Step 8
acceptance_criteria.then: the literal string 'baseline_head_sha' appears inside the dispatch payload block (not only in a comment), AND 'baseline_dirty_snapshot' also appears in the same block

check.expect: match in dispatch payload body, not comment-only

acceptance_criteria.when: a reader reads the dev.files_modified and dev.files_created field descriptions
acceptance_criteria.then: the description explicitly states: dev.files_modified is derived from 'git diff --name-only <baseline_head_sha>' (working-tree diff against the baseline SHA); AND dev.files_created is derived from 'git ls-files --others --exclude-standard' excluding paths that appeared as untracked entries in baseline_dirty_snapshot; AND the schema includes an 'observed_preexisting[]' field with description; AND 'baseline_head_sha' appears as a top-level dev-report JSON field

check.expect: all four patterns present in Output Format section

acceptance_criteria.given: agents/qa.md with a provenance check section
acceptance_criteria.when: a reader reads the provenance check procedure
acceptance_criteria.then: the text specifies: (a) compute git diff --name-only <baseline_head_sha> (working tree vs baseline); for every path in dev.files_modified absent from this diff AND absent from baseline_dirty_snapshot, raise a critical finding with primary_cause: 'dev_implementation' and label 'dev_provenance_violation'; AND (b) compute git ls-files --others --exclude-standard; for every path in dev.files_created absent from this untracked list AND absent from baseline_dirty_snapshot, raise a critical finding with primary_cause: 'dev_implementation' and label 'files_created_provenance_violation'

check.expect: all five patterns present in provenance check section

acceptance_criteria.when: a reader reads the dev-report enrichment procedure
acceptance_criteria.then: the text specifies a split provenance filter: (a) for dev.files_modified — absent from 'git diff --name-only <baseline_head_sha>' AND not in baseline_dirty_snapshot gives provenance_anomaly; (b) for dev.files_created — absent from 'git ls-files --others --exclude-standard' AND not present as a untracked entry in parsed baseline_dirty_snapshot gives provenance_anomaly; AND the text explicitly states that the '..HEAD' form must NOT be used because Phase 2 runs before commit; AND provenance_anomaly paths are excluded from commit-message type/scope/summary derivation

check.expect: provenance_anomaly, baseline_head_sha, ls-files --others, and Phase 2 pre-commit note present; git diff with ..HEAD form absent as an executable command (may appear in explanatory text as a refutation)

acceptance_criteria.given: a dev-report where baseline_head_sha is null or absent
acceptance_criteria.when: QA and changelog-analyst read their respective provenance check/classification rules
acceptance_criteria.then: the text in both agents/qa.md and agents/changelog-analyst.md specifies that the provenance check is skipped with a warning (not FAIL) when baseline_head_sha is absent

check.expect: graceful skip / warning language when baseline_head_sha is absent

acceptance_criteria.given: agents/qa.md with a provenance check section
acceptance_criteria.when: a reader reads the provenance check procedure for dev.files_created
acceptance_criteria.then: the text specifies 'git ls-files --others --exclude-standard' as the mechanism for detecting legitimate new (untracked) files, AND uses the label 'files_created_provenance_violation' for paths in dev.files_created absent from the untracked list AND absent from baseline_dirty_snapshot

check.expect: both patterns present in the files_created provenance check subsection
